# Cocoa / Export Commodities Trader Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-cocoa-exporter-cocoa-export-trader
**Vertical slug:** cocoa-exporter
**VN-ID:** VN-AGR-EXP-001
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-EXP (standalone agricultural export)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria is Africa's second-largest cocoa producer (estimated 250,000–280,000 MT/year), concentrated in Ondo, Osun, Cross River, and Ogun states. Export traders (licensed buying agents — LBAs) aggregate cocoa from cooperatives and sell to international buyers (Barry Callebaut, Olam, Cargill). The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) and Cocoa Association of Nigeria (CAN) regulate the sector. NAFDAC certifies processed cocoa products. Phytosanitary certificates from NAQS (Nigerian Agricultural Quarantine Service) are mandatory for export. Other exported commodities include sesame, cashew, ginger, shea butter, and palm kernel. B2B WhatsApp and email are the primary buyer communication channels.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **International buyer (Ghana buyer office), 45**: Looking for 500 MT cocoa beans, grade 1, moisture ≤7.5%. Needs NAQS phytosanitary cert, fumigation records, SGS/Bureau Veritas quality report, and NEPC export permit.
- **Cooperative society leader Mama Biodun, 58, Ondo**: Representing 300 cocoa farmers. Wants trusted LBA for offtake at fair price. Needs payment proof and NCDC licence.
- **Shea butter processor Mr Tanko, 40, Kano**: Sources raw shea nuts from aggregators and processes for EU cosmetics market. Needs NAFDAC and NAQS-certified supplier.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (trader name, "NEPC Licensed Cocoa Export Trader"), WhatsApp enquiry CTA, featured commodities (cocoa, sesame, cashew, shea — with grade specs and price indications), trust strip (NEPC, CAN, NAQS, CAC)
- **About**: Company profile, CAN/NCDC licence, export history (volume/destinations), international buyer relationships
- **Commodities**: Cocoa grades, sesame specs, cashew grades — all with quality certifications, MOQ, and WhatsApp enquiry
- **Contact**: WhatsApp trade enquiry (primary), office address, buyer registration form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Professional, international-standard, and compliance-forward. "NEPC Licensed. Quality Certified", "From Nigerian Farms to Global Markets", "CAN Member. NAQS Compliant". Primary CTA: "Submit a Buying Enquiry via WhatsApp". Secondary: "View Commodity Specifications". Avoid: generic commodity trading imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NEPC (Nigerian Export Promotion Council) export licence
- CAN (Cocoa Association of Nigeria) membership
- NCDC (Nigerian Cocoa Development Committee) licence
- NAQS phytosanitary certificate
- SGS/Bureau Veritas quality inspection reference
- Fumigation compliance certificate
- International buyer references (anonymised)
- NAFDAC certificate for processed derivatives

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Cocoa farm harvest — Nigerian farmer with cocoa pods, Ondo or Cross River State
2. Quality inspection — trader checking cocoa grade, moisture meter, warehouse
3. Export warehouse — sacks of cocoa ready for shipment, NEPC sticker, fumigation marker
4. Port loading — containers being loaded at Apapa/Tin Can Port, export context
5. Phytosanitary certificate — NAQS document, international compliance signal
6. Cocoa pod cross-section — beans and pulp, quality/grade focus
7. Farmer-trader handshake — rural cooperative meeting, fair trade context

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] NEPC export licence
- [ ] CAN membership certificate
- [ ] NCDC buying agent licence
- [ ] NAQS phytosanitary certificate
- [ ] Fumigation compliance documentation
- [ ] SGS/Bureau Veritas quality inspection records
- [ ] NDPR consent on buyer registration form

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp trade enquiry CTA prominent
- [x] NEPC/CAN/NAQS trust signals
- [x] Nigerian cocoa-producing states referenced (Ondo, Cross River, Osun)
- [x] International grade specifications (grade 1, moisture %, defect %)
- [x] Nigerian commodity references (cocoa, sesame, cashew, shea)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on buyer registration form
